summary: A hybrid soundboard/audience matrix (slightly low‑pitch) captures a solid show highlighted by the marathon Scarlet>Fire and strong versions of They Love Each Other, Samson & Delilah, Playin' in the Band and Black Peter.
review: The Miller_113354 recording is a mixed‑source (soundboard with audience patches) that runs a hair slow and sounds dry, requiring about +1 % pitch correction for the long Scarlet>Fire segment. Musically the concert is an average to good outing from Brent’s first year – the 33½‑minute Scarlet>Fire dominates, while They Love Each Other, Samson & Delilah, Playin' in the Band and Black Peter receive particular praise.

ai_rating.rationale: Show receives mixed feedback (average 3‑star rating) with several standout songs, while recording is decent but suffers from pitch and dryness issues; combined weighting yields a modest overall score.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Hybrid soundboard‑audience source; SBD portion is dry and runs ~1 % slow, requiring pitch correction for the Scarlet>Fire through Terrapin. Audio patches are clean but overall mix lacks fullness.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set opens strong with Jack Straw and a smoking They Love Each Other but settles into an average middle; second set is dominated by the epic Scarlet>Fire leading into tight performances of Samson, Playin' in the Band and Black Peter, ending with a spirited Good Lovin'.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Shaky on the Scarlet>Fire opening but praised for his nuanced approach on They Love Each Other.
band_member_comments.Phil: Less highlighted; reviewers felt the Rhythm Devils outshone Phil in this show.
band_member_comments.Keys: Early Brent era noted; keyboards provide period flavor though no specific praise is given.
band_member_comments.Drums: Unique drumming intro on Looks Like Rain and strong Rhythm Devil work on Good Lovin'.
